name: Swamp Azalea
summary: Fragrant white-pink azalea of acid wet edges — vernal pool margins, peat hollows, and seep edges. Blooms June–July (later than pinxter azalea), extending the spring native azalea forage window by a full month.
notes: Hosts azalea-specialist bee Andrena cornelli. Special value to bumblebees. Night flowers are sphinx-moth pollinated; daytime visited by hummingbirds and swallowtails. Honest fit for Pocono acid wet sites. Pair with pinxter azalea (dry upland) for sequential native azalea bloom across the spring/early-summer.

planting.aspect: Vernal pool edges; wet hollows; seep margins
planting.site_notes: Plant only on permanently or seasonally wet acid sites. Wire cage; deer browse moderately. Pinelands Nursery and Mellow Marsh Farm carry this species reliably.
